title: Savory Tangy Slovakian Tofu Dumplings
description: A romantic Slovakian dish featuring tangy and chewy tofu dumplings, this recipe is vegan-friendly and incorporates pecorino for a unique flavor, the dumplings are first brine-dried to enhance their texture and taste.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cornstarch, nutritional yeast, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Add the crumbled tofu, pecorino cheese alternative, lemon juice, and apple cider vinegar to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk shape and place them on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Brush the tops of the dumplings with olive oil.
Bake the dumplings in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, then broil for an additional 2 minutes to dry the surface.
Remove the dumplings from the oven and let them cool on a wire rack for 10 minutes.
Serve the dumplings hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
